prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "行进弓步" (walking lunge) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person mid-stride during a walking lunge: front leg (right) just landed with foot flat and knee bent at 90 degrees, thigh parallel to the floor; rear leg (left) extended back with knee bent and hovering just above the ground, ball of the rear foot on the ground; torso upright with neutral spine, hands on hips, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the front leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hamstring group of the front leg and hip flexors of the rear leg. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps of the front leg; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "腘绳肌 / 髂腰肌" pointing to the hamstring and hip flexor area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
